nodes.context: Ecuadorian criminal and terrorist organization specializing in drug trafficking and hitman services.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos began as a splinter group of the Los Choneros drug cartel, but broke away in 2020 after the death of Jorge Luis Zambrano
edges.context: Los Lobos originated as a splinter group of Los Choneros.

edges.evidence_quote: a dispute between Los Lobos and Los Choneros in the Litoral Penitentiary, Guayaquil, led to a riot that killed 123 inmates
edges.context: Los Lobos and Los Choneros fought in a prison riot.

edges.evidence_quote: broke away in 2020 after the death of Jorge Luis Zambrano, along with Los Chone Killers and Los Tiguerones
edges.context: Los Lobos and Los Chone Killers broke away from Los Choneros together.

edges.evidence_quote: broke away in 2020 after the death of Jorge Luis Zambrano, along with Los Chone Killers and Los Tiguerones
edges.context: Los Lobos and Los Tiguerones broke away from Los Choneros together.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Lobos, along with their ally Los Tiguerones, were behind a wave of violence across Ecuador
edges.context: Los Lobos and Los Tiguerones cooperated in a wave of violence.

edges.evidence_quote: Alexander Quesada, the leader of Los Lobos, and Marcelo Anchundia, the leader of rival gang R7, were reportedly behind the riot
edges.context: Los Lobos and R7 are rival gangs involved in prison riots.

edges.evidence_quote: The riot was fought between members of Los Lobos and R7 in an attempt to kill Anchundia
edges.context: Los Lobos and R7 fought in a prison riot.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers, Kompania Bello, FARC 48th Front, Tren de Aragua
edges.context: Los Lobos is allied with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers, Kompania Bello, FARC 48th Front, Tren de Aragua
edges.context: Los Lobos is allied with Kompania Bello.
